rationale: Post-Υ-D doctrine now freezes live trust-transition prerequisites, live-cutover receipt and provenance generalization, and publication/trust operationalization gates.

Existing shard and net substrate already exposes deterministic shard placement, lifecycle logging, cross-shard message logging, checkpoint-aware runtime state, shard version/baseline hashes, and typed ownership-transfer events.
Φ-B5 can therefore extend real repo surfaces and explicit admission law instead of inventing greenfield distributed-authority meaning.
The exact blocker family identified by the prior checkpoint is now materially addressed by Υ-D0..Υ-D2.
Any remaining Υ-D3-style work would be mostly consolidation rather than a prerequisite doctrine layer.
Trust-transition admission, live-cutover receipt continuity, and publication/trust operationalization are now explicit.
Remaining Ζ blockers are concentrated in distributed authority semantics, deterministic replication proof, distributed replay continuity, runtime cutover proof, and later live-system realization.
The pre-Φ-B5 trust and cutover admission layer is now explicit in Υ-D0..Υ-D2.
The shard/net substrate provides real precursor authority, lifecycle, checkpoint, routing, and ownership-transfer surfaces for Φ-B5 to extend.
Manual review remains FULL for distributed authority model changes and trust-root governance changes.
Older derived readiness surfaces remain conservative about Ζ live operations, but do not override the active checkpoint law for a doctrine-level Φ-B5 prompt.
cautions: Ready status applies to doctrine-level distributed authority foundations only.
This does not admit distributed runtime implementation, shard relocation, quorum execution, or live trust convergence.
Φ-B5 must preserve hotswap boundaries, replay, snapshot, isolation, coexistence, and the post-Υ-D admission model.

rationale: Execution envelope, rehearsal, rollback alignment, canary, trust execution continuity, live trust prerequisites, live-cutover receipt generalization, and operationalization gates are already explicit.
Any further controller-specific prompt is no longer a prerequisite to doctrine-level distributed authority foundations.
Υ-D0..Υ-D2 already embody the final pre-Φ-B5 admission distinctions the prior checkpoint required.
Another narrow band before Φ-B5 would mostly restate explicit law.

zeta_blockers_or_prerequisites.reason: Φ-B5 has not yet frozen lawful authority handoff, proof-anchor quorum, or distributed authority convergence semantics.
Replication policy and shard substrate remain precursor surfaces rather than proof-backed replication and partition-transfer law.
Replay, checkpoint, and cross-shard logs exist, but distributed replay verification and proof-anchor continuity across handoff are still unresolved.
Hotswap boundaries are explicit, but distributed runtime cutover proof remains unproven and must stay subordinate to those boundaries.
Υ-D0..Υ-D2 define admission and continuity law, but not the distributed convergence model itself.
Trust-transition prerequisites and operationalization gates are explicit, but later live trust, revocation, and publication systems remain unimplemented.
Generalized live-cutover receipt and provenance law is explicit, but later live-system realization is still absent.

must_avoid_replacing: canonical ownership-sensitive roots with convenience roots
runtime boundary law with controller or tooling folklore
trust truth with publication, mirror, archive, or local verifier convenience
active checkpoint law with stale numbering or older planning drift

ownership_projection_cautions: fields/ remains canonical semantic field substrate while field/ remains transitional
schema/ remains canonical semantic contract law while schemas/ remains projection-facing
packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging and activation scope while data/packs/ remains scoped authored-pack authority
canonical prose and semantic specs outrank projected or generated mirrors
the thin runtime/ root is not canonical by name alone
release, archive, mirror, publication, trust, distributed, and tooling convenience must not infer canon or permission

reason: Υ-D0..Υ-D2 close the final narrow admission and continuity band the prior checkpoint required.
The repo now has both explicit admission law and real shard/net precursor substrate for a doctrine-level distributed authority foundations prompt.
Another pre-Φ-B5 narrow band would mostly consolidate already-explicit law rather than reduce a distinct blocker family.

stability.replacement_target: later post-Φ-B5 checkpoint may refine readiness judgments without replacing the checkpoint law frozen here
